commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4322db2e7e1ef7cc08f033bf41e735712272d8dd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates paired-device `lastSeenAtMs`/`lastSeenReason` on accepted device-token auth and paired WebSocket reconnects, with focused device-pairing and gateway regression tests.

PR surface: Source +11, Tests +20. Total +31 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes from source: pair a device, authenticate with an accepted device token or reconnect, and current main updates token/access metadata without refreshing paired-device `lastSeenAtMs`/`lastSeenReason`; I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is conflict resolution and maintainer review on a dirty branch, not a narrow automated repair to the reviewed diff.

target.title: fix(devices): refresh paired device last-seen metadata
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #81169.
- Refreshes paired-device `lastSeenAtMs` / `lastSeenReason` when accepted device-token auth succeeds, using the current `createdAtMs` / `lastSeenAtMs` contract.
- Marks successful paired WebSocket reconnects as `lastSeenReason: "connect"` through the existing paired metadata update.
- Does not revive legacy `createdAt` / `lastSeen` names, weaken scope checks, change pairing approval, or relax token verification.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Accepted paired-device auth now refreshes device-level last-seen metadata so stale paired clients can be audited.

Real environment tested:
OpenClaw source at this PR head, using an isolated temporary pairing state directory.

Exact command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --eval '<isolated pairing proof script>'
```

Evidence after fix, with device token redacted:

```json
{
  "proof": "paired device last-seen refresh on accepted device-token auth",
  "before": {
    "tokenPresent": true,
    "lastSeenAtMs": null,
    "lastSeenReason": null
  },
  "auth": {
    "ok": true,
    "reason": null,
    "token": "<redacted>",
    "requestedScopes": [
      "operator.read"
    ]
  },
  "after": {
    "lastSeenAtMsType": "number",
    "lastSeenReason": "device-token-auth",
    "tokenLastUsedAtMsType": "number"
  },
  "redactions": [
    "device token"
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The accepted device-token auth path returns `ok: true`, writes numeric `lastSeenAtMs`, records `lastSeenReason: "device-token-auth"`, and keeps token material redacted in proof output.

What was not tested:
No physical mobile client or installed user state was used for this proof.

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/infra/device-pairing.test.ts src/gateway/server.auth.control-ui.suite.ts src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/device-pairing.ts src/gateway/server/ws-connection/message-handler.ts src/infra/device-pairing.test.ts src/gateway/server.auth.control-ui.suite.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (no actionable correctness issues found; no follow-up fixes needed)
